key_insight: LLM judges are much less reliable at grading real student math reasoning than synthetic solutions, revealing a large evaluation gap between artificial and authentic reasoning.
engineering_relevance: For math education and assessment pipelines, this means benchmark results on synthetic outputs may overestimate judge quality and should not be trusted as a proxy for real student work.
method: Built RealMath-Eval from 224 real high-school exam responses with expert human grading.
Compared state-of-the-art LLM judges on real student solutions versus synthetic LLM-generated solutions.
Used embedding, surprisal, and style-transfer analyses to explain why the judges generalize poorly to human reasoning.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports high judge error on real student responses (MSE ~2.96) versus much lower error on synthetic solutions (MSE ~1.17).
watch_item: The benchmark is relatively small and exam-domain specific, so broader claims about all math reasoning tasks should be made cautiously.
paper_title: RealMath-Eval: Why SOTA Judges Struggle with Real Human Reasoning
